task.title: Implement a Handler
task.shortdesc: Implement an execution handler when you introduce a custom action type or custom mutation semantics.

prereq.p: Define the action type emitted by your plugin and decide whether core handlers can execute it.
For first-time plugin onboarding, implement dry-run behavior before filesystem writes so you can validate dispatch safely.

step.cmd: Create a handler class that inherits from ExecutionHandler and defines action_type.
Implement parameter validation, source-root validation, and dry-run behavior.
Enforce sandbox and mutation policy before writing files.
Return ExecutionActionResult with structured status and metadata.
Register the handler class from handlers() and run execution tests.

result.p: Your action type has a deterministic execution implementation with explicit failure reporting.
result.codeblock: handler tests show: success case returns status=success dry-run case returns status=skipped failure case returns status=failed with error details
